content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 23, 2016

Marina Castillo-Augusto, M.S.
Chief, Community Development
& Engagement Unit
Office of Health Equity
CA Department of Public Health
1615 Capitol Avenue
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. A-16-054

Dear Ms. Castillo-Augusto:

     This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Health Program Specialist I Kurt
Schweigman regarding his duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").¹ Note that the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act
as a finder of fact when it renders advice. Un re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Additionally, our
advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We offer no opinion on the applicability of other
laws, such as common law conflict of interest.

QUESTION

     In light of Mr. Schweigman's prior contacts with organizations that have and will be
submitting proposals for the California Reducing Disparities Project (the "Project"), may
Mr. Schweigman participate in his official capacity in the Project's mandatory bidder's conference
and oversee the contracts/grantees that are funded under the Project?

CONCLUSION

     So long as Mr. Schweigman has no financial interest in any of the persons submitting
proposals on the project, he will not have a conflict of interest.

FACTS

     You are the Chief of the Community Development and Engagement Unit with the Office of
Health Equity at the California Department of Public Health. Recently you hired Mr. Schweigman

___________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-16-054
Page No. 2

as a Health Program Specialist I ("HPS I"). Mr. Schweigman, in his position of HPS I, will oversee
contracts/grants under the Project. Mr. Schweigman used to work for and/or interfaced with the
California Indian Rural Health Board, The Native American Health Center, Friendship House and
other Native American organizations that have and will be submitting proposals for the Project.
Mr. Schweigman has not received any payments/wages from these organizations within the last 12
months or longer.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. Section 82048 defines "public official" as every member, officer,
employee, or consultant of a state or local government agency. Mr. Schweigman is a public official
under the Act and a designated employee of the California Department of Public Health.

A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one or more of the official's
interests. (Section 87103.) Section 87103 of the Act lists several types of financial interests that can
give rise to a conflict of interest; the ones most relevant to your situation are:

• Sources of Income -- A public official has a financial interest in any source of income,
including promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the
decision. (Section 87103(c).)

• Gifts -- A public official has a financial interest in any donor of a gift or gifts aggregating
$460 or more in value, if the gift is provided or promised to the official within 12 months
prior to a governmental decision. (Section 87103(e).)

According to your facts, Mr. Schweigman does not have any of the financial interests recognized by
the Act as a basis for disqualification. However, financial interests can be acquired at any time.
Therefore, he should be cognizant of what constitutes a disqualifying financial interest, as well as
the ramifications of acquiring one.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla Wagner
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

and oversee the contracts/grantees that are funded under the Project?

proposals on the project, he will not have a conflict of interest.
